title: Ensure Private Endpoints are used to access Storage  Accounts

input_row.title: Ensure Private Endpoints are used to access Storage  Accounts
input_row.description: Use private endpoints for your Azure Storage accounts to allow clients and services to securely access data located over a network via an encrypted Private Link. To do this, the private endpoint uses an IP address from the VNet for each service. Network traffic between disparate services securely traverses encrypted over the VNet. This VNet can also link addressing space, extending your network and accessing resources on it. Similarly, it can be a tunnel through public networks to connect remote infrastructures together. This creates further security through segmenting network traffic and preventing outside sources from accessing it.
input_row.rationale: Securing traffic between services through encryption protects the data from easy interception and reading.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Open the Storage Accounts blade. 2. For each listed Storage Account, perform the following check: 3. Under the Security + networking heading, click on Networking. 4. Click on the Private endpoint connections tab at the top of the networking window. 5. Ensure that for each VNet that the Storage Account must be accessed from, a unique Private Endpoint is deployed and the Connection state for each Private Endpoint is Approved. Repeat the procedure for each Storage Account. Audit from PowerShell $storageAccount = Get-AzStorageAccount -ResourceGroup '' - Name '' Get-AzPrivateEndpoint -ResourceGroup ''|Where-Object {$_.PrivateLinkServiceConnectionsText -match $storageAccount.id} If the results of the second command returns information, the Storage Account is using a Private Endpoint and complies with this Benchmark, otherwise if the results of the second command are empty, the Storage Account generates a finding. Audit from Azure CLI az storage account show --name '' --query "privateEndpointConnections[0].id" If the above command returns data, the Storage Account complies with this Benchmark, otherwise if the results are empty, the Storage Account generates a finding. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 6edd7eda-6dd8-40f7-810d-67160c639cd9 - Name: 'Storage accounts should use private link'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Open the Storage Accounts blade 2. For each listed Storage Account, perform the following: 3. Under the Security + networking heading, click on Networking 4. Click on the Private endpoint connections tab at the top of the networking window 5. Click the + Private endpoint button 6. In the 1 - Basics tab/step: o Enter a name that will be easily recognizable as associated with the Storage Account (Note: The "Network Interface Name" will be automatically completed, but you can customize it if needed.) o Ensure that the Region matches the region of the Storage Account o Click Next 7. In the 2 - Resource tab/step: o Select the target sub-resource based on what type of storage resource is being made available o Click Next 8. In the 3 - Virtual Network tab/step: o Select the Virtual network that your Storage Account will be connecting to o Select the Subnet that your Storage Account will be connecting to o (Optional) Select other network settings as appropriate for your environment o Click Next 9. In the 4 - DNS tab/step: o (Optional) Select other DNS settings as appropriate for your environment o Click Next 10. In the 5 - Tags tab/step: o (Optional) Set any tags that are relevant to your organization o Click Next 11. In the 6 - Review + create tab/step: o A validation attempt will be made and after a few moments it should indicate Validation Passed - if it does not pass, double-check your settings before beginning more in depth troubleshooting. o If validation has passed, click Create then wait for a few minutes for the scripted deployment to complete. Repeat the above procedure for each Private Endpoint required within every Storage Account. Remediate from PowerShell $storageAccount = Get-AzStorageAccount -ResourceGroupName '' -Name '' $privateEndpointConnection = @{ Name = 'connectionName' PrivateLinkServiceId = $storageAccount.Id GroupID = "blob|blob_secondary|file|file_secondary|table|table_secondary|queue|queue_se condary|web|web_secondary|dfs|dfs_secondary" } $privateLinkServiceConnection = New-AzPrivateLinkServiceConnection @privateEndpointConnection $virtualNetDetails = Get-AzVirtualNetwork -ResourceGroupName '' -Name '' $privateEndpoint = @{ ResourceGroupName = '' Name = '' Location = '' Subnet = $virtualNetDetails.Subnets[0] PrivateLinkServiceConnection = $privateLinkServiceConnection } New-AzPrivateEndpoint @privateEndpoint Remediate from Azure CLI az network private-endpoint create --resource-group -- location --name --vnet-name -- subnet --private-connection-resource-id -- connection-name --group-id
input_row.impact: If an Azure Virtual Network is not implemented correctly, this may result in the loss of critical network traffic. Private endpoints are charged per hour of use. Refer to https://azure.microsoft.com/en- us/pricing/details/private-link/ and https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/calculator/ to estimate potential costs.
input_row.default_value: By default, Private Endpoints are not created for Storage Accounts.
input_row.additional_information: A NAT gateway is the recommended solution for outbound internet access. This recommendation is based on the Common Reference Recommendation Ensure Private Endpoints are used to access {service}, from the Common Reference Recommendations > Networking > Private Endpoints section.

step1_initial_assessment:
### AUDIT APPROACH: Automated

### PROGRAM NAME:
aws_ec2_s3_bucket_private_endpoint_used

### AUTOMATION DETAILS:
This automated program will check for the presence of Private Endpoints configured for S3 buckets, aligning with the control focused on ensuring secure access to storage accounts through Private Link.

1. **AWS API Calls or boto3 SDK Methods to use:**
   - Use `boto3.client('ec2')` to interact with the EC2 service related to the Private Link.
   - Use `boto3.client('s3')` to check bucket configurations.
   - The specific method to check private link connections would typically involve VPC endpoint functionalities.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - To list VPC endpoints for S3: 
     ```
     aws ec2 describe-vpc-endpoints --filters "Name=service-name,Values=com.amazonaws.<region>.s3"
     ```

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - List all S3 buckets across accounts or specific regions.
   - List all VPC endpoints and filter for S3 endpoints.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - For each S3 bucket, check if a private endpoint exists in the connected VPC.
   - Validate the `PrivateLinkServiceConnections` property to ensure endpoints are appropriately approved.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass Criteria:** Each S3 bucket has an associated private endpoint, and the connection status is "Approved."
   - **Fail Criteria:** If the private endpoint is absent or the connection status is anything other than "Approved," it fails the compliance check.

6. **Example Clarification:**
   - Use `boto3` methods to iteratively check each S3 bucket:
     ```python
     s3_buckets = s3.list_buckets()
     for bucket in s3_buckets['Buckets']:
         # Check for corresponding VPC endpoint details
         # Apply logic to determine compliance
     ```

This approach ensures automated ongoing compliance checks on the Private Endpoint usage for S3 buckets, which enhances security by reducing the risk of exposure through public endpoints.
step2_review:
### APPROACH VALIDATION: PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is appropriate since AWS APIs and CLI can be used to check for VPC endpoints associated with S3 buckets.
- **AWS API/boto3/CLI Feasibility:** The approach can indeed be automated using AWS APIs and the AWS CLI. However, the original control is from Azure, and the translation to AWS services should be more precise, focusing on AWS-specific configurations.

### TECHNICAL ACCURACY: 6

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are partially correct. The use of `boto3.client('ec2')` to describe VPC endpoints is appropriate, but the focus should be on `boto3.client('ec2').describe_vpc_endpoints()` to check for S3-specific endpoints.
- **boto3 Methods/CLI Commands:** The CLI command provided is correct for listing VPC endpoints. However, the boto3 example lacks specific methods for checking the association between S3 buckets and VPC endpoints.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_ec2_s3_bucket_private_endpoint_used` is not entirely descriptive or clear. It should reflect the specific security check, such as `aws_s3_vpc_endpoint_check`.

### IMPROVEMENTS:

- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_s3_vpc_endpoint_check` to better reflect the focus on S3 and VPC endpoints.
- **Missing Considerations:** 
  - The assessment should include checking the endpoint policy to ensure it restricts access appropriately.
  - Consider using AWS Config rules like `s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited` and `s3-bucket-public-write-prohibited` to complement the endpoint checks.
- **Alternative AWS API Methods:** Use `boto3.client('s3control').get_bucket_policy_status()` to ensure bucket policies align with private endpoint usage.
- **Clarification on PrivateLinkServiceConnections:** This property does not exist in AWS. Instead, focus on the `VpcEndpoint` configuration.

### CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM

- The assessment shows a good understanding of AWS capabilities but lacks precision in translating Azure-specific controls to AWS services. The technical details need refinement to ensure accurate compliance checks.

### FINAL RECOMMENDATION: Modify

- **Modify the Automation Details:** Refine the boto3 and CLI commands to accurately reflect the association between S3 buckets and VPC endpoints. Ensure the use of correct AWS terminology and methods.
- **Program Name Change:** Update the program name to `aws_s3_vpc_endpoint_check` for clarity and specificity.
- **Enhance Technical Details:** Include endpoint policy checks and consider AWS Config rules for comprehensive compliance validation.
